overview: UX refinements for SE Schedule based on user feedback. Desktop: sticky headers, scroll-sync date picker, minimum column widths, calendar popup, font changes, short card fixes, modal/card styling. Mobile: scroll vs tap interaction fix, person switcher ordering, custom styled dropdowns, time picker component. General: more mock data, 15-min default events, conflict detection on create, view/edit mode split.

userStories.title: Replace Bebas Neue with Uppercase Open Sans
userStories.description: Remove Bebas Neue font entirely from the project. Replace all instances where Bebas Neue was used with Open Sans in uppercase. Update index.html to remove Bebas Neue Google Font import. Update tailwind.config.js to remove font-heading. Update index.css base styles. Update all components using font-heading class to use font-body with uppercase styling instead.
acceptanceCriteria: Bebas Neue removed from Google Fonts link in index.html
tailwind.config.js no longer references Bebas Neue or font-heading
index.css h1-h6 styles use Open Sans with text-transform: uppercase
All components previously using font-heading now use font-body uppercase
Visual appearance maintains heading hierarchy through size/weight, not font family
npm run lint passes
Verify in browser at mobile (375px) and desktop (1280px)

userStories.title: Add Two Weeks of Mock Event Data
userStories.description: Extend src/data/events.json with additional mock events covering the two weeks following the existing data (2026-02-06 through 2026-02-20). Maintain realistic distribution: techs get jobs, sales get sales-stops, all get some meetings. Include varied statuses. Follow existing title conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: events.json contains events for 2026-02-06 through 2026-02-20
At least 5-8 events per day across all team members
Tech schedules: mostly job-occupied, job-vacant, some callbacks and meetings
Sales schedules: mostly sales-stops, some meetings
Ops schedule: mostly meetings
Status distribution maintained: ~60% open, ~25% closed-no-invoice, ~15% closed-invoiced
Event titles follow conventions (apartment names for jobs, company names for sales)
JSON parses without errors
Events display correctly in both mobile and desktop views

userStories.title: Change Default New Event Duration to 15 Minutes
userStories.description: Update all places where new events are created to default to 15-minute duration instead of 1 hour. This affects: FAB create flow, long-press slot create, desktop click-to-create, and any other event creation paths. Update CreateEventModal defaults and all handlers that set default end time.
acceptanceCriteria: FAB → create event defaults to start time + 15 minutes for end time
Long-press on mobile slot defaults to 15-minute duration
Desktop click-to-create defaults to 15-minute duration
CreateEventModal default endTime is startTime + 15 minutes
All event creation paths use 15-minute default
Verify in browser: create event via FAB, long-press, desktop click

userStories.title: Fix Short Event Card Display
userStories.description: Improve EventCard component to handle very short events (15-30 minutes) better. For 15-minute events, only show the title in a single truncated line. For events shorter than 45 minutes, use condensed layout. Ensure text doesn't overflow the card bounds. Make the left color strip wider (6px instead of 4px). Make status indicator dot bigger (10px instead of 8px).
acceptanceCriteria: 15-minute events show only title, single line, truncated with ellipsis
Events under 45 minutes use condensed layout (title + time only)
Events 45+ minutes show full layout (title, time, type)
Left color border is 6px wide (was 4px)
Status indicator dot is 10px diameter (was 8px)
No text overflow or clipping issues at any event duration
Verify in browser with events of 15, 30, 45, and 60+ minute durations

userStories.title: Add Conflict Detection on Event Creation
userStories.description: Add conflict detection when creating new events via the CreateEventModal. Before saving, check if the new event overlaps with any existing events for the same person on the same date. If conflict exists, show an error toast and prevent saving. Reuse the existing conflict detection logic from drag-and-drop.
acceptanceCriteria: CreateEventModal checks for conflicts before calling onSave
Conflict check uses same logic as drag-and-drop (time overlap for same person/date)
Toast error displayed when conflict detected: 'Cannot create event - conflicts with existing event'
Modal stays open when conflict detected (user can adjust times)
No conflict = event saves normally
Works for both mobile and desktop create modals
Verify by trying to create overlapping events

userStories.title: Split Event Click into View Details and Edit Mode
userStories.description: Refactor event click behavior. Clicking an event now opens a View Details modal/panel showing event info (read-only). Add a pencil icon button to enter edit mode. Add a 'View full details' link (non-functional placeholder). Edit mode shows the existing edit form. This applies to both mobile and desktop.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking event card opens EventDetailsModal (new component)
EventDetailsModal shows: title, type with color indicator, assignee name, date, start-end time, status
Pencil icon button in top-right of details modal
Clicking pencil icon transitions to edit mode (EditEventModal)
'View full details' link displayed at bottom (no href, just text link style)
Mobile: details as bottom sheet, edit as bottom sheet
Desktop: details as centered modal, edit as centered modal
Back/close from edit returns to details view
Verify in browser on both mobile and desktop

userStories.title: Fix Mobile Scroll vs Tap Interaction Conflict
userStories.description: Currently on mobile, scrolling the calendar is blocked because tap interactions capture all touches. Fix this by: (1) removing the tap-on-empty-slot-to-create behavior (keep only long-press), (2) ensuring normal scroll works, (3) tap on event card still opens details. The long-press to create should still work.
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile calendar grid scrolls normally with finger drag
Single tap on empty slot does NOT create an event (removed)
Long-press (500ms) on empty slot still opens create modal
Single tap on event card opens event details
Long-press on event card also opens event details
No pointer-events or touch-action CSS blocking scroll
Verify in browser at mobile width: scroll up/down, tap events, long-press empty slots

userStories.title: Fix Person Switcher Ordering and Highlight
userStories.description: Update TeamMemberSwitcher component. Mike Torres (tm-1) should always appear at the bottom of the list, directly above the FAB button, and always be highlighted as the 'current user' regardless of which schedule is being viewed. Other team members should be sorted alphabetically by first name. The selected member (whose schedule is shown) gets the orange selection indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: Mike Torres (tm-1) always appears at bottom of member list
Mike Torres row always has the 'current user' styling (his color background on FAB)
Other team members sorted alphabetically by first name (David, James, Jennifer, Rachel, Sarah)
Currently viewed member has orange accent indicator (ring or dot)
Mike Torres can be both 'current user' AND 'selected' (both indicators)
FAB button always shows Mike Torres's initials/color
Verify in browser: open switcher, check order, switch members, confirm indicators

userStories.title: Custom Styled Dropdowns in Event Modal
userStories.description: Replace native select elements in CreateEventModal and EditEventModal with custom styled dropdown components. Style should match the dark chrome design system: dark background (#2A2A2A), white text, orange accent on selected/hover, rounded corners. Dropdown options should be scrollable if too many.
acceptanceCriteria: Event Type dropdown uses custom styled component, not native select
Person dropdown uses custom styled component
Dropdown trigger shows current selection with down arrow
Dropdown menu: dark bg (#2A2A2A), white text, rounded-lg
Hover state: slightly lighter bg or orange accent
Selected option: orange indicator or checkmark
Event Type dropdown shows color dots next to each option
Dropdowns close on selection or outside click
Works on both mobile and desktop modals
Verify in browser: open modal, interact with dropdowns

userStories.title: Replace Time Dropdowns with Time Picker Component
userStories.description: Replace the start time and end time select dropdowns in event modals with a custom time picker component. Design: scrollable hour/minute wheels or a grid of time slots. Must enforce 15-minute increments. Range: 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM. Style to match design system.
acceptanceCriteria: Start Time uses custom TimePicker component
End Time uses custom TimePicker component
TimePicker shows time in 12-hour format with AM/PM
Only 15-minute increments selectable (6:00, 6:15, 6:30, 6:45, etc.)
Time range limited to 6:00 AM - 8:00 PM
Styled to match design system: dark theme, orange accents
Picker is touch-friendly on mobile (large tap targets)
Works in both CreateEventModal and EditEventModal
Verify in browser: open modal, select times, confirm values

userStories.title: Make Desktop Date Picker and Column Headers Sticky
userStories.description: Ensure the DesktopDatePicker bar and the team member column headers remain fixed/sticky at the top of the viewport when scrolling the calendar. Date picker should be at the very top, column headers directly below it. Both should have proper z-index layering.
acceptanceCriteria: DesktopDatePicker stays fixed at top of main content area during scroll
Team member column headers stay sticky below the date picker
Headers don't overlap or flicker during scroll
z-index layering correct: date picker > column headers > day separators > content
Day separator headers still sticky within their sections (below column headers)
Smooth scroll behavior maintained
Verify in browser at desktop width: scroll through multiple days, headers stay fixed

userStories.title: Date Picker Arrows Scroll to Day Sections
userStories.description: Update DesktopDatePicker so that clicking the left/right arrows not only changes the selected date but also scrolls the calendar view to that day's section. Ensure smooth scroll animation. This should work bidirectionally (arrows update scroll position, scrolling updates date picker).
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking left arrow scrolls calendar to previous day's section
Clicking right arrow scrolls calendar to next day's section
Scroll animation is smooth (not instant jump)
Date picker display updates to match scrolled-to day
Manual scrolling still updates date picker (existing behavior)
Today button scrolls to today's section
Verify in browser: click arrows, observe smooth scroll to correct day

userStories.title: Add Minimum Column Width with Horizontal Scroll
userStories.description: Set a minimum width for team member columns in the desktop grid (e.g., 150px). If the total width of all columns exceeds the viewport, enable horizontal scrolling. This ensures columns remain readable even with many team members.
acceptanceCriteria: Each team member column has minimum width of 150px
With current 6 members at 150px min = 900px total + time labels
If viewport is narrower than total column width, horizontal scroll enabled
Horizontal scroll affects only the grid area, not the date picker
Column headers scroll horizontally in sync with grid content
Time labels column stays fixed on the left during horizontal scroll
Verify in browser: resize to narrow width, confirm horizontal scroll appears

userStories.title: Add Calendar Date Picker Popup
userStories.description: Add a calendar popup when clicking the date text in DesktopDatePicker. Clicking the date (e.g., 'Monday, February 3, 2026') opens a month calendar view. User can click any date to navigate to it. Calendar styled to match design system. Clicking outside closes it.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking date text in DesktopDatePicker opens calendar popup
Calendar shows month view with clickable day cells
Current selected date highlighted with orange
Today's date has subtle indicator if different from selected
Month/year navigation arrows in calendar header
Clicking a date: closes popup, updates date picker, scrolls grid to that day
Clicking outside popup closes it
Calendar styled: dark bg, white text, orange accents
Verify in browser: click date, navigate calendar, select date

userStories.title: Fix Desktop Event Card Click Opening Modal
userStories.description: Debug and fix the issue where clicking an event card on desktop does not open the event details/edit modal. Ensure click handlers are properly attached and not blocked by drag handlers. The DraggableEvent wrapper should allow clicks through to the EventCard or handle them appropriately.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking event card on desktop opens EventDetailsModal
Click works on first attempt (no need to double-click)
Drag still works: press and move starts drag, click without move opens modal
Click handlers not blocked by @dnd-kit listeners
Console shows no errors on click
Works for all event types and durations
Verify in browser at desktop width: click various events, confirm modal opens

userStories.title: Polish and Final Testing
userStories.description: Final polish pass for all changes. Test all new functionality end-to-end. Fix any visual inconsistencies. Ensure all acceptance criteria from previous stories are still working. Run linting and build checks.
acceptanceCriteria: All fonts are Open Sans (no Bebas Neue)
Mock data displays correctly for full 3-week range
15-minute default events work everywhere
Short event cards display correctly
Conflict detection works on create
View Details → Edit flow works on both mobile and desktop
Mobile scroll works, long-press creates, tap opens details
Person switcher shows correct order and highlights
Custom dropdowns and time pickers work
Desktop sticky headers work
Date picker arrows scroll correctly
Calendar popup works
Desktop event click opens modal
npm run lint passes
npm run build succeeds
Verify in browser at 375px, 768px, 1280px viewports
